Defines the ECMA built-in Math object with number constants and mathematical function routines.

# Purpose
This code defines the mathematical constants and functions for a built-in `Math` object, as specified in the ECMAScript Language Specification (ECMA-262, 5th edition). It uses macros to define various properties and routines associated with the `Math` object. The `OBJECT_ID` macro assigns an identifier to the `Math` object, while the `NUMBER_VALUE` macro defines several mathematical constants such as `E`, `LN10`, `LN2`, `LOG2E`, `LOG10E`, `PI`, `SQRT1_2`, and `SQRT2`, each with fixed property attributes.

The `ROUTINE` macro defines a set of mathematical functions that the `Math` object provides. These functions include `abs`, `acos`, `asin`, `atan`, `atan2`, `ceil`, `cos`, `exp`, `floor`, `log`, `max`, `min`, `pow`, `random`, `round`, `sin`, `sqrt`, and `tan`. Each routine is associated with a corresponding C function, the number of arguments it accepts, and the length property value. This code is intended to be part of a larger system where these macros are expanded to implement the `Math` object's functionality in a JavaScript engine.
